annotations.title: Clean install
annotations.detail: `pip install flyte` completes without errors. The package is distributed as a wheel on PyPI, so no build toolchain is required.

annotations.title: 54 packages installed
annotations.detail: 54 transitive packages is within acceptable range (threshold: 80). Notable heavy transitive deps include grpcio, protobuf, pydantic, and cryptography (via keyring).

annotations.title: Import time: 0.31s
annotations.detail: 0.31s import time is acceptable for an SDK of this scope. Most of the time is likely spent importing grpcio and protobuf stubs.

annotations.title: README example crashes: `_LocalRun` has no `result` attribute
annotations.detail: The first Python example in the package METADATA (README) calls `run.result` on line 95, but the `_LocalRun` class (defined in `flyte/_run.py:640-674`) does not expose a `result` property. The correct API is `run.outputs()` which returns an `ActionOutputs` tuple subclass. To get the scalar result: `run.outputs()[0]` or `run.outputs().o0`. This is a documentation bug shipped in the 2.0.7 METADATA, meaning every new user hitting PyPI or `pip show` will encounter broken example code on their first try.

annotations.title: ActionOutputs API is non-obvious
annotations.detail: `flyte.run()` returns a `_LocalRun` whose `outputs()` method returns `ActionOutputs`, a `tuple` subclass. Results are accessed positionally (`outputs[0]`) or via auto-generated names (`outputs.o0`, `outputs.named_outputs['o0']`). This is not documented in the README and would require reading source code to discover after hitting the `AttributeError`.

annotations.title: No quickstart recovery path
annotations.detail: After the README example fails, there is no `--help` hint, error suggestion, or link to docs in the traceback. A new user must read source code or search GitHub issues to find the correct API. The SDK could improve this by adding a `__getattr__` on `_LocalRun` that raises a helpful error like "Did you mean .outputs()?".

annotations.title: CLI --version works
annotations.detail: `flyte --version` returns `Flyte SDK version: 2.0.7`. The CLI entry point is registered correctly via `rich-click`.

annotations.title: CLI --help works
annotations.detail: `flyte --help` outputs well-formatted documentation powered by `rich-click`. Subcommands are clearly listed.

annotations.title: 20 direct dependencies
annotations.detail: The package declares 20 direct runtime dependencies including heavyweight libraries: grpcio, protobuf, pydantic, httpx, keyring, cloudpickle, fsspec, and obstore. This is heavy for a first install and increases the surface area for version conflicts in existing projects.

annotations.title: `flyteidl2==2.0.7` exact pin risks conflicts
annotations.detail: `flyteidl2` is pinned to exactly `2.0.7` (lock-step with the SDK version). This means any environment that needs a different flyteidl2 version (e.g., for another Flyte-ecosystem tool) will hit an immediate resolver conflict. Lock-step pins are common for protobuf IDL packages but create friction in monorepos or shared virtualenvs.

annotations.title: `rich-click==1.8.9` exact pin risks conflicts
annotations.detail: `rich-click` is pinned to exactly `1.8.9`. Any other package in the environment requiring a different rich-click version will cause pip to fail. This pin is likely due to API compatibility concerns but should ideally be a compatible-release constraint (`~=1.8.9`) instead of an exact pin.

annotations.title: 54 total packages in venv
annotations.detail: Transitive dependency count is 54, well under the 80 threshold.
